expectations.text: Review does NOT include a complexity/split advisory
expectations.evidence: No complexity advisory present. The review correctly sizes this as a small change.

expectations.text: Review does not flag breaking changes
expectations.evidence: No breaking changes are mentioned in the review.
The Recommendation section says 'The two high-severity bugs affect blocks with root-level properties in the routed view. Fix before merging.' This is effectively a Request Changes verdict.
Review is 48 lines, well under the 200 line limit.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering
expectations.evidence: Finding 'Minor - getViewAliasForTab helper not used in render method' is a code style/consistency nitpick about using a helper vs inline construction. Finding 'Minor - #provideViewContext hard-throws when view not found' is debatable but leans toward a style preference (graceful degradation vs throwing).
